verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait David in corde suo : Aliquando incidam una die in manus Saul : nonne melius est ut fugiam, et salver in terra Philisthinorum, ut desperet Saul, cessetque me quærere in cunctis finibus Israël ? fugiam ergo manus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר דָּוִד֙ אֶל־לִבּ֔וֹ עַתָּ֛ה אֶסָּפֶ֥ה יוֹם־אֶחָ֖ד בְּיַד־שָׁא֑וּל אֵֽין־לִ֨י ט֜וֹב כִּ֣י הִמָּלֵ֥ט אִמָּלֵ֣ט ׀ אֶל־אֶ֣רֶץ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֗ים וְנוֹאַ֨שׁ מִמֶּ֤נִּי שָׁאוּל֙ לְבַקְשֵׁ֤נִי עוֹד֙ בְּכׇל־גְּב֣וּל יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וְנִמְלַטְתִּ֖י מִיָּדֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said in his heart: I shall one day or other fall into the hands of Saul: is it not better for me to flee, and to be saved in the land of the Philistines, that Saul may despair of me, and cease to seek me in all the coasts of Israel? I will flee then out of his hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin David ajatteli sydämessänsä: kuitenkin minä yhtenä päivänä joudun Saulin käsiin: ei ole minun parempaa, kuin että minä menen ojetinsa Philistealaisten maalle, että Saul lakkais minua etsimästä kaikissa Israelin rajoissa, ja minä pääsisin hänen käsistänsä.
verses.king_james: And David said in his heart, I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul: there is nothing better for me than that I should speedily escape into the land of the Philistines; and Saul shall despair of me, to seek me any more in any coast of Israel: so shall I escape out of his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surrexit David, et abiit ipse, et sexcenti viri cum eo, ad Achis filium Maoch regem Geth.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣קׇם דָּוִ֔ד וַיַּעֲבֹ֣ר ה֔וּא וְשֵׁשׁ־מֵא֥וֹת אִ֖ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עִמּ֑וֹ אֶל־אָכִ֥ישׁ בֶּן־מָע֖וֹךְ מֶ֥לֶךְ גַּֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David arose, and went away, both he and the six hundred men that were with him, to Achis, the son of Maoch, king of Geth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David nousi ja läksi matkaan kuudensadan miehen kanssa, jotka hänen myötänsä olivat, Akiksen Maokin pojan Gatin kuninkaan tykö.
verses.king_james: And David arose, and he passed over with the six hundred men that were with him unto Achish, the son of Maoch, king of Gath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et habitavit David cum Achis in Geth, ipse et viri ejus : vir et domus ejus : et David, et duæ uxores ejus, Achinoam Jezrahelitis, et Abigail uxor Nabal Carmeli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֩שֶׁב֩ דָּוִ֨ד עִם־אָכִ֥ישׁ בְּגַ֛ת ה֥וּא וַאֲנָשָׁ֖יו אִ֣ישׁ וּבֵית֑וֹ דָּוִד֙ וּשְׁתֵּ֣י נָשָׁ֔יו אֲחִינֹ֙עַם֙ הַיִּזְרְעֵאלִ֔ת וַֽאֲבִיגַ֥יִל אֵֽשֶׁת־נָבָ֖ל הַֽכַּרְמְלִֽית׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David dwelt with Achis at Geth, he and his men; every man with his household, and David with his two wives, Achinoam, the Jezrahelitess, and Abigail, the wife of Nabal of Carmel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin David asui Akiksen tykönä Gatissa, hän ja hänen miehensä itsekukin perheinensä: David ja kaksi hänen emäntäänsä, Ahinoam Jisreelista ja Abigail Nabalin emäntä Karmelista.
verses.king_james: And David dwelt with Achish at Gath, he and his men, every man with his household, even David with his two wives, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, and Abigail the Carmelitess, Nabal’s wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nuntiatum est Sauli quod fugisset David in Geth, et non addidit ultra quærere eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֻּגַּ֣ד לְשָׁא֔וּל כִּֽי־בָרַ֥ח דָּוִ֖ד גַּ֑ת וְלֹא־[יָסַ֥ף] (יוסף) ע֖וֹד לְבַקְשֽׁוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And it was told Saul that David was fled to Geth, and he sought no more after him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Saulille sanottiin Davidin paenneen Gatiin, niin ei hän enää häntä etsinyt.
verses.king_james: And it was told Saul that David was fled to Gath: and he sought no more again for him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem David ad Achis : Si inveni gratiam in oculis tuis, detur mihi locus in una urbium regionis hujus, ut habitem ibi : cur enim manet servus tuus in civitate regis tecum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד אֶל־אָכִ֗ישׁ אִם־נָא֩ מָצָ֨אתִי חֵ֤ן בְּעֵינֶ֙יךָ֙ יִתְּנוּ־לִ֣י מָק֗וֹם בְּאַחַ֛ת עָרֵ֥י הַשָּׂדֶ֖ה וְאֵ֣שְׁבָה שָּׁ֑ם וְלָ֨מָּה יֵשֵׁ֧ב עַבְדְּךָ֛ בְּעִ֥יר הַמַּמְלָכָ֖ה עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Achis: If I have found favour in thy sight, let a place be given me in one of the cities of this country, that I may dwell there: for why should thy servant dwell in the royal city with thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin David sanoi Akikselle: jos minä olen löytänyt armon sinun edessäs, niin anna minulle yhteen kaupunkiin sia maalle, asuakseni siellä; sillä miksi pitäis sinun palvelias asuman kuninkaallisessa kaupungissa sinun tykönäs?
verses.king_james: And David said unto Achish, If I have now found grace in thine eyes, let them give me a place in some town in the country, that I may dwell there: for why should thy servant dwell in the royal city with thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedit itaque ei Achis in die illa Siceleg : propter quam causam facta est Siceleg regum Juda usque in diem hanc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֶּן־ל֥וֹ אָכִ֛ישׁ בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא אֶת־צִֽקְלָ֑ג לָכֵ֞ן הָיְתָ֤ה צִֽקְלַג֙ לְמַלְכֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֔ה עַ֖ד הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Then Achis gave him Siceleg that day: for which reason Siceleg belongeth to the kings of Juda unto this day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä päivänä antoi Akis hänelle Ziklagin. Sentähden on Ziklag Juudan kuningasten vielä tänäpänä.
verses.king_james: Then Achish gave him Ziklag that day: wherefore Ziklag pertaineth unto the kings of Judah unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuit autem numerus dierum quibus habitavit David in regione Philisthinorum, quatuor mensium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ מִסְפַּ֣ר הַיָּמִ֔ים אֲשֶׁר־יָשַׁ֥ב דָּוִ֖ד בִּשְׂדֵ֣ה פְלִשְׁתִּ֑ים יָמִ֖ים וְאַרְבָּעָ֥ה חֳדָשִֽׁים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the time that David dwelt in the country of the Philistines, was four months.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan se aika, minkä David asui Philistealaisten maalla, oli ajastaika ja neljä kuukautta.
verses.king_james: And the time that David dwelt in the country of the Philistines was a full year and four months.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendit David et viri ejus, et agebant prædas de Gessuri, et de Gerzi, et de Amalecitis : hi enim pagi habitabantur in terra antiquitus, euntibus Sur usque ad terram Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַל דָּוִד֙ וַאֲנָשָׁ֔יו וַֽיִּפְשְׁט֛וּ אֶל־הַגְּשׁוּרִ֥י (והגרזי) [וְהַגִּזְרִ֖י] וְהָעֲמָלֵקִ֑י כִּ֣י הֵ֜נָּה יֹשְׁב֤וֹת הָאָ֙רֶץ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר מֵעוֹלָ֔ם בּוֹאֲךָ֥ שׁ֖וּרָה וְעַד־אֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David and his men went up, and pillaged Gessuri, and Gerzi, and the Amalecites: for these were of old the inhabitants of the countries, as men go to Sur, even to the land of Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David nousi miehinensä ja karkasi Gessurilaisten ja Girsiläisten ja Amalekilaisten maalle; sillä nämät asuivat jo vanhuudesta siinä maassa, kuin Suriin mennään, Egyptin maahan asti.
verses.king_james: And David and his men went up, and invaded the Geshurites, and the Gezrites, and the Amalekites: for those nations were of old the inhabitants of the land, as thou goest to Shur, even unto the land of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et percutiebat David omnem terram, nec relinquebat viventem virum et mulierem : tollensque oves, et boves, et asinos, et camelos, et vestes, revertebatur, et veniebat ad Achis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִכָּ֤ה דָוִד֙ אֶת־הָאָ֔רֶץ וְלֹ֥א יְחַיֶּ֖ה אִ֣ישׁ וְאִשָּׁ֑ה וְלָקַח֩ צֹ֨אן וּבָקָ֜ר וַחֲמֹרִ֤ים וּגְמַלִּים֙ וּבְגָדִ֔ים וַיָּ֖שׇׁב וַיָּבֹ֥א אֶל־אָכִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David wasted all the land, and left neither man nor woman alive: and took away the sheep, and the oxen, and the asses, and the camels, and the apparel, and returned and came to Achis.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin David oli kaiken maan lyönyt, ei hän jättänyt yhtäkään miestä eli vaimoa elämään; ja otti heidän lampaansa, härkänsä, aasinsa, kamelinsa ja vaatteensa, palasi ja tuli Akiksen tykö.
verses.king_james: And David smote the land, and left neither man nor woman alive, and took away the sheep, and the oxen, and the asses, and the camels, and the apparel, and returned, and came to Achish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem ei Achis : In quem irruisti hodie ? Respondebat David : Contra meridiem Judæ, et contra meridiem Jerameel, et contra meridiem Ceni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אָכִ֔ישׁ אַל־פְּשַׁטְתֶּ֖ם הַיּ֑וֹם וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֗ד עַל־נֶ֤גֶב יְהוּדָה֙ וְעַל־נֶ֣גֶב הַיְּרַחְמְאֵלִ֔י וְאֶל־נֶ֖גֶב הַקֵּינִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Achis said to him: Whom hast thou gone against to day? David answered: Against the south of Juda, and against the south of Jerameel, and against the south of Ceni.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Akis sanoi: kenenkä päälle te olette karanneet tänäpänä? David vastasi: lounaan päin Juudaa, lounaan päin Jerakmelilaisia ja lounaan päin Keniläisiä.
verses.king_james: And Achish said, Whither have ye made a road to day? And David said, Against the south of Judah, and against the south of the Jerahmeelites, and against the south of the Kenites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Virum et mulierem non vivificabat David, nec adducebat in Geth, dicens : Ne forte loquantur adversum nos : Hæc fecit David : et hoc erat decretum illi omnibus diebus quibus habitavit in regione Philisthinorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֨ישׁ וְאִשָּׁ֜ה לֹא־יְחַיֶּ֣ה דָוִ֗ד לְהָבִ֥יא גַת֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר פֶּן־יַגִּ֥דוּ עָלֵ֖ינוּ לֵאמֹ֑ר כֹּֽה־עָשָׂ֤ה דָוִד֙ וְכֹ֣ה מִשְׁפָּט֔וֹ כׇּ֨ל־הַיָּמִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָשַׁ֖ב בִּשְׂדֵ֥ה פְלִשְׁתִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David saved neither man nor woman, neither brought he any of them to Geth, saying: Lest they should speak against us. So did David, and such was his proceeding all the days that he dwelt in the country of the Philistines.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ei David jättänyt eläväksi yhtäkään miestä eli vaimoa viedäksensä Gatiin, ja ajatteli: ettei he puhuisi jotakin meitä vastaan, sanoen: näin teki David, ja tämä oli hänen tapansa niinkauvan kuin hän asui Philistealaisten maalla.
verses.king_james: And David saved neither man nor woman alive, to bring tidings to Gath, saying, Lest they should tell on us, saying, So did David, and so will be his manner all the while he dwelleth in the country of the Philistines.

verses.latin_vulgate: Credidit ergo Achis David, dicens : Multa mala operatus est contra populum suum Israël : erit igitur mihi servus sempiternus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּאֲמֵ֥ן אָכִ֖ישׁ בְּדָוִ֣ד לֵאמֹ֑ר הַבְאֵ֤שׁ הִבְאִישׁ֙ בְּעַמּ֣וֹ בְיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וְהָ֥יָה לִ֖י לְעֶ֥בֶד עוֹלָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Achis believed David, saying: He hath done much harm to his people Israel: Therefore he shall be my servant for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Akis uskoi Davidin ja ajatteli, hän on tehnyt itsensä kokonansa kauhistuttavaksi kansallensa Israelille, sentähden olkaan hän aina minun palveliani.
verses.king_james: And Achish believed David, saying, He hath made his people Israel utterly to abhor him; therefore he shall be my servant for ever.
